name: SDLC Review Reject Twice Live Test Cycle
description: Test-owned SDLC playbook that proves two reviewer request-changes loops before approval.
outcome: Production-ready software with named greetings, uppercase output, unsupported invalid-option rejection with a usage message, and invalid-option regression coverage that proves at least two distinct unsupported long-option cases with the exact usage text on stderr, a non-zero exit, and no stdout output, plus two deliberate review rework cycles before approval.

definition.process_instructions: The live-test product manager clarifies the goal and acceptance criteria. For this profile, the authored requirements MUST explicitly include that unsupported `--*` flags are rejected with the documented usage message and that invalid-option regression coverage proves at least two distinct unsupported long-option cases, each asserting the exact usage text on stderr, a non-zero exit, and no stdout output. The live-test architect produces the technical design before implementation and MUST carry the same unsupported-flag rejection and explicit invalid-option regression closure into the design. The live-test developer implements the change. On the first two implementation reviews, the live-test reviewer MUST produce concrete request-changes verdicts with actionable feedback in the review output and handoff, using a `full` structured handoff with `resolution=request_changes` so the review result is explicit and the orchestrator routes the required rework. Those request-changes handoffs MUST populate structured `remaining_items` and `review_focus` entries so the developer receives precise rework direction through the normal continuity surfaces. After two real rework cycles have happened, approve the corrected resubmission when those structured findings are addressed. Do not invent broader invalid-option requirements beyond the authored closure bar for this profile. The orchestrator is responsible for routing the resulting rework back to the developer task each time. The live-test QA role validates evidence after reviewer approval. This flow has no human approval gates. When a checkpoint deliverable is accepted and the next checkpoint begins, complete the finished checkpoint work item instead of leaving it open.

columns.label: Planned
columns.description: Work is defined but not yet assigned.

columns.label: In Review
columns.description: Primary work is complete and under review.

columns.label: Blocked
columns.description: Work is blocked and needs intervention.

columns.label: Done
columns.description: The work item meets its acceptance criteria.

stages.name: requirements
stages.goal: Approved requirements with testable acceptance criteria

stages.name: design
stages.goal: Approved system design with key decisions captured

stages.name: implementation
stages.goal: Working code with tests on a feature branch

stages.name: review
stages.goal: Reviewer verdict with concrete findings or approval

stages.name: release
stages.goal: Operator-ready release package and final release summary

checkpoints.name: requirements
checkpoints.goal: Approved requirements with testable acceptance criteria.
checkpoints.entry_criteria: The workflow goal and initial context are available.

checkpoints.name: design
checkpoints.goal: A design exists for the implementation approach when needed.
checkpoints.entry_criteria: Requirements are clear enough to shape implementation.

checkpoints.name: implementation
checkpoints.goal: Working code exists and is ready for formal review.
checkpoints.entry_criteria: Requirements and any required design are available.

checkpoints.name: review
checkpoints.goal: A reviewer verdict exists for every developer-delivered change.
checkpoints.entry_criteria: Implementation output and developer handoff are available.

checkpoints.name: verification
checkpoints.goal: QA validation and evidence are complete.
checkpoints.entry_criteria: Required review is complete and approved.

checkpoints.name: release
checkpoints.goal: Release notes, residual risk, and operator summary are complete.
checkpoints.entry_criteria: Verification evidence and operator summary are ready.
